title: magnistipula butayei subsp. tisserantii
intro: Magnistipula butayei subsp. tisserantii is a plant species, also known as the Philippine white oak, which is native to the Philippines and belongs to the family Magncipulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Magnistipula butayei subsp. tisserantii include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by boiling the bark and leaves in water to create a decoction that can be used to treat various ailments.
